title: Diasperus teysmannii
intro: Diasperus teysmannii, also known as Java tea tree, is a plant species native to Indonesia, belonging to the family Rutaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, and has been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, digestive issues, and skin conditions. Bioactive constituents of Diasperus teysmannii include flavonoids, such as naringenin and hesperidin, and limonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Diasperus teysmannii is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
taxonomy: Diasperus teysmannii is classified within the family Apocynaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This classification places Diasperus teysmannii within the order Gentianales. Within the Apocynaceae family, Diasperus teysmannii is believed to be part of the subfamily Rauvolfioideae. Further classification places Diasperus teysmannii within the tribe Rauvolfieae. The exact species classification of Diasperus teysmannii may be subject to change as new research emerges.
